title: Credit Plan Term and Condition
description: Credit Plan Terms and Conditions define the contractual rules, eligibility, obligations, and constraints that govern a credit plan (loan, credit card, overdraft, or financing product).

PrincipalRule.description:
Defines the allowed structure of the borrowed amount.

Includes:
Minimum loan amount
Maximum loan amount
Step increments
Tenor (minimum and maximum duration)
Currency constraints

Comment:
Example:
Min €10,000  Max €500,000  Tenor 530 years.

InterestRule.description:
Defines how interest is calculated, accrued, and applied.

Includes:
Interest type (Fixed, Variable, Floating)
Base rate reference (e.g., market index)
Margin/spread
Compounding frequency
Accrual basis (Actual/360, 30/360, etc.)
Default/penalty interest
Compound Interest Concept: Total accumulated amount after compounding interest.

FeeRule.description:
Total accumulated amount after compounding interest.

Includes:
Processing fee
Origination fee
Administration fee
Late payment fee
Early repayment penalty
Fee calculation method (Fixed / % / Tiered)

Comment:
Example:
1% processing fee deducted at disbursement.

RepaymentRule.description:
Defines how and when the borrower repays the loan.

Includes:
Repayment type (Annuity, Equal Principal, Bullet)
Installment frequency (Monthly, Quarterly)
Grace period
Moratorium rules
Prepayment conditions
Payment allocation logic (interest first, then principal)
Annuity Payment Formula: Calculates equal periodic installments.

DisbursementRule.description:
Defines how loan funds are released to the borrower.

Includes:
Single or multiple tranches
Linked settlement account
Conditional release
Deadline for drawdown

Comment:
Example:
Construction loan released in 4 stages

CollateralRule.description:
Defines required security backing the loan

Includes:
Collateral type (Property, Vehicle, Guarantee)
Loan-to-Value (LTV) limit
Valuation requirement
Insurance requirement

Comment:
Example:
Max 80% LTV for residential mortgage.

Risk&EligibilityRule.description:
Defines borrower qualification criteria.

Includes:
Minimum credit score
Income threshold
Debt-to-income ratio
Employment criteria
Risk category mapping
Approval authority level

Comment:
Example:
DTI must not exceed 40%.

Default&DelinquencyRule.description:
Defines what happens if borrower fails to pay.

Includes:
Days past due definition
Default trigger threshold
Penalty interest activation
Acceleration clause
Collection process
Write-off rules

Comment:
Example:
Loan classified as default after 90 days past due.

Legal&RegulatoryTerm.description:
Defines contractual and regulatory obligations.

Includes:
Governing law
Jurisdiction
Consumer protection clauses
AML/KYC requirements
Data privacy terms
Regulatory classification

Operational&LifecycleRule.description:
Defines how the credit plan behaves over time.

Includes:
Amendment rules
Restructuring conditions
Renewal terms
Transfer rules
Early termination process
Closure procedures

x-relationships.description: Inherits from Credit Plan Product Feature
